title: phegopteris inaequalis
intro: Phegopteris inaequalis, also known as the unequal-leaved lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to parts of Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Phegopteris inaequalis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
